files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+name: CI
+
+on:
+  push:
+  pull_request:
+
+permissions:
+  contents: read
+
+jobs:
+  test:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
+        with:
+          go-version-file: go.mod
+          check-latest: true
+
+      - name: gofmt
+        run: |
+          test -z "$(gofmt -l .)"
+
+      - name: go test
+        run: |
+          go test ./...
+
+      - name: golangci-lint
+        uses: golangci/golangci-lint-action@v6
+        with:
+          version: latest
+          args: --timeout=5m
+

@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+# --- Go ---
+# Binaries
+delicli
+*.exe
+*.dll
+*.so
+*.dylib
+
+# Test / coverage output
+*.test
+*.out
+coverage.txt
+cover.out
+
+# Build dirs (if used)
+bin/
+dist/
+build/
+
+# Go workspace (optional)
+go.work
+go.work.sum
+
+# --- macOS ---
+.DS_Store
+.AppleDouble
+.LSOverride
+._*
+.Spotlight-V100
+.Trashes
+
+# --- Editors ---
+.idea/
+.vscode/
+*.swp
+*.swo

@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+run:
+  timeout: 5m
+
+linters:
+  disable-all: true
+  enable:
+    - errcheck
+    - govet
+    - ineffassign
+    - staticcheck
+    - unused
+
+issues:
+  max-issues-per-linter: 0
+  max-same-issues: 0
+

@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+.PHONY: fmt lint test ci
+
+fmt:
+	gofmt -w .
+
+lint:
+	golangci-lint run
+
+test:
+	go test ./...
+
+ci: fmt lint test
+

@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+# delicli
+
+Simple order watcher for JSON endpoints/files.
+
+This repo intentionally keeps things lightweight and does not do any auth bypass or scraping. If you want a CLI view of *your* order updates, point `delicli` at a JSON file you control (or at an HTTP endpoint you’re allowed to call).
+
+## Build
+
+```bash
+go build ./cmd/delicli
+```
+
+## Dev
+
+Requires Go `1.25.x` (see `go.mod`).
+
+```bash
+make fmt
+make lint
+make test
+make ci
+```
+
+## Try it
+
+```bash
+./delicli test
+```
+
+## Watch a file (JSON or JSONL)
+
+```bash
+./delicli watch --file ./examples/sample.jsonl
+```
+
+## Fetch once (HTTP)
+
+```bash
+./delicli get --url http://localhost:8080/order/status
+```
+
+Basic auth for endpoints you control:
+
+```bash
+echo 'mypassword' | ./delicli get --url http://localhost:8080/order/status --username me --password -
+```
+
+Raw JSON output:
+
+```bash
+./delicli get --url http://localhost:8080/order/status --json --pretty
+```
+
+## Deliveroo (convenience)
+
+If you have permission and valid credentials/session for Deliveroo’s consumer APIs, `delicli` includes convenience commands that build the URLs for you. You must provide auth via headers (no bypass).
+
+```bash
+export DELIVEROO_MARKET=uk
+export DELIVEROO_BEARER_TOKEN="…"
+
+./delicli deliveroo orders
+./delicli deliveroo active
+./delicli deliveroo watch-active --interval 5s
+```
+
+Auth/config options:
+
+- Env: `DELIVEROO_MARKET`, `DELIVEROO_BEARER_TOKEN`, `DELIVEROO_COOKIE`
+- Flags: `--market`, `--base-url`, `--bearer-token`, `--cookie`, `--header`, `--include-ugc`
+
+## JSON shape (generic)
+
+`delicli` stays generic and just tries common keys like `order_id`, `status`, `eta`, `updated_at` to print a summary line. Use `--json` if you want to see the full payload.
+
+For a sample payload, run `./delicli test --json --pretty`.

@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+module delicli
+
+go 1.25
+
+toolchain go1.25.5
+
+require (
+	github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5 v5.3.0
+	golang.org/x/term v0.38.0
+)
+
+require golang.org/x/sys v0.39.0 // indirect

@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+package deliveroo
+
+type OrdersResponse struct {
+	Orders []Order `json:"orders"`
+	Count  int     `json:"count"`
+}
+
+type Order struct {
+	ID                  string      `json:"id"`
+	OrderNumber         string      `json:"order_number"`
+	Status              string      `json:"status"`
+	StatusTimestamp     string      `json:"status_timestamp"`
+	OrderType           string      `json:"order_type"`
+	PaymentStatus       string      `json:"payment_status"`
+	EstimatedDeliveryAt string      `json:"estimated_delivery_at"`
+	DeliveredAt         string      `json:"delivered_at"`
+	SubmittedAt         string      `json:"submitted_at"`
+	Total               *float64    `json:"total"`
+	OriginalTotal       *float64    `json:"original_total"`
+	CurrencySymbol      string      `json:"currency_symbol"`
+	CurrencyCode        string      `json:"currency_code"`
+	Restaurant          *Restaurant `json:"restaurant"`
+}
+
+type Restaurant struct {
+	Name string `json:"name"`
+}
